title: pyrus pollwylleriana
intro: Pyrus pollwylleriana, also known as the Japanese rowan or fire pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Japan. Its bark and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Pyrus pollwylleriana include tea infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
